page: Amazon Web Services r8idn.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores powered by Intel processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, this instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor to deliver dedicated compute resources with minimal virtualization overhead.

The instance is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For high-speed local data access, it bundles 22800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering substantial capacity directly attached to the physical host.

Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 300 Gbps, which is optimized for both network traffic and Elastic Block Store (EBS) throughput. This hardware profile is designed for large-scale databases, real-time in-memory analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads requiring high-throughput local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idn.96xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 384 dedicated vCPUs and 192 physical cores powered by Intel x86_64 processors running at 3.9 GHz. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance provides 3072.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 22800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. Designed without GPU accelerators, the instance is optimized for high-throughput network and EBS performance. This resource-dense profile is engineered for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale databases, and real-time in-memory analytics that require substantial local storage capacity and high network bandwidth.

tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel processors, massive local NVMe storage, and high-speed baseline network performance.

3072.0 GB of system memory for memory-intensive applications
